title: clarification_interaction_schema
description: Schema for Class 2 clarification interaction artifacts: bounded candidate choices, presentation channel, user/caregiver selection, timeout/no-response result, and transition target. This schema does not define pure context input, validator approval, actuation command, doorlock authorization, or emergency trigger authority. Optional fixture metadata fields are allowed for integration-test artifacts but do not create operational authority. For timeout/no-response artifacts, candidate_choices should preserve the choices that were presented or pending at the time of timeout so that timeout outcomes remain auditable.

payload_family.description: Optional fixture/runtime metadata describing the clarification interaction payload family. This field is not policy authority.

schema_ref.description: Optional fixture metadata pointing to this schema.

clarification_id.description: Unique identifier for this clarification interaction.

audit_correlation_id.description: Correlation ID linking this clarification interaction to audit logs and upstream events.

source_scenario.description: Optional scenario identifier when this payload is used in integration fixtures or tests.

source_layer.description: System layer that created or updated this clarification interaction artifact. caregiver_confirmation_backend may update the artifact when caregiver confirmation provides selection evidence, but this does not create autonomous actuation authority.

unresolved_reason.description: Machine-readable reason for entering or remaining in Class 2 clarification.

candidate_source.description: Provenance of the candidate set. llm_generated means LocalLlmAdapter.generate_class2_candidates() produced the candidates within bounded-variability constraints; default_fallback means Class2ClarificationManager._DEFAULT_CANDIDATES was used (e.g. on LLM failure or constraint violation); static_only_forced means the experiment runner explicitly disabled the LLM via routing_metadata.class2_candidate_source_mode='static_only' (Package A LLM-vs-static comparison; doc 10 §3.3 P2.3) — distinct from default_fallback so audit can tell 'forced static' apart from 'LLM tried and failed'. Optional field for backward compatibility; absence means default_fallback for legacy records.

candidate_choices.description: Bounded choices that may be presented to the user/caregiver for confirmation. Candidate choices are guidance only and do not authorize actuation. For timeout/no-response artifacts, this array should preserve the choices that were presented or pending at the time of timeout.

presentation_channel.description: Channel used to present candidate choices or clarification prompt.

selection_result.description: Result of user/caregiver selection. Omit when no selection has been collected yet.

transition_target.description: Resulting transition target after confirmation, deterministic evidence, timeout, or pending state.

timestamp_ms.description: Creation or update timestamp in epoch milliseconds.

notes.description: Optional fixture or implementation note. Notes are not operational authority.

input_mode.description: Optional. Class 2 interaction model used for this session. 'direct_select' (default) presents all candidates in one TTS utterance and accepts a selected_candidate_id; 'scanning' presents one option at a time and accepts {option_index, response} per turn. Single-mode legacy records may omit (treated as 'direct_select'). Plan: 12_class2_scanning_input_mode_plan.md.

scan_history.description: Optional. Per-option decisions during a scanning session (input_mode='scanning'). Empty / absent for direct_select. Final entry's response='yes' identifies the accepted option (otherwise the session escalated). Plan: 12_class2_scanning_input_mode_plan.md §5.3.

option_index.description: 0-based index into the session's candidate_choices that this turn addressed.

candidate_id.description: candidate_id of the option presented in this turn.

response.description: User answer for this turn. 'silence' = per-option timeout (treated as 'no' for advancing). 'dropped' = a stale or out-of-order input was received and discarded; current_option_index unchanged.

elapsed_ms.description: Time the system waited on this option before recording the response.

refinement_history.description: Optional. Each entry records one refinement turn that occurred between the initial user selection and the terminal Class 2 outcome. Plan reference: 11_class2_multi_turn_refinement_plan.md. Single-turn records (the production default) omit this field; opt-in multi-turn records carry exactly one entry today (max one refinement turn).

turn_index.description: 1-based turn index. The initial user selection is turn 0 (implicit); the first refinement turn is 1.

parent_candidate_id.description: candidate_id of the option the user picked in the previous turn that triggered this refinement.

refinement_question.description: The question that was announced to the user for this refinement turn. Subject to the same max_prompt_length_chars cap as initial prompts.

selected_candidate_id.description: candidate_id chosen during this refinement turn, OR a synthetic value (e.g. 'TIMEOUT') when the turn timed out.

selection_source.description: Where the refinement selection came from (e.g. 'user_mqtt_button', 'timeout').

selection_timestamp_ms.description: Wall-clock ms when the refinement selection was recorded.
